payload.executiveSummary: The lecture framed the chapter accurately and used precise terminology, but students needed a stronger checkpoint to show they could apply the concept without additional prompting.
whatWentWell: The instructor tied the chapter language back to the assigned text.
Examples stayed aligned to the textbook framing instead of drifting into unrelated review.
Terminology was defined before the more abstract comparison task.
whatCanImprove: The lecture needed a stronger student-facing check before moving on.
A misconception contrast would have helped clarify the key distinction in the chapter.
The close of the lesson did not confirm which students could explain the idea independently.
payload.recommendedNextStep: Add a short written checkpoint tied directly to the textbook prompt so the instructor can separate passive recognition from real conceptual understanding.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Key Findings
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Textbook alignment was visible throughout the lesson.
bullets: Examples stayed inside the selected chapter frame.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Missed Opportunities
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Students needed a more concrete application check.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Student Engagement Signals
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Student thinking was mostly inferred from instructor prompts.

instructionalCoachingFeedback.label: Suggested Next Steps
instructionalCoachingFeedback.content: Pair the next explanation with a short applied writing task.

higherEdAlignment.label: Textbook Alignment
higherEdAlignment.content: The lesson stayed connected to the assigned textbook chapter.

higherEdAlignment.label: Missing Conceptual Depth
higherEdAlignment.content: Students needed a stronger application checkpoint before moving on.

higherEdAlignment.label: Terminology Precision
higherEdAlignment.content: Terminology stayed accurate and appropriately framed for the course level.

higherEdAlignment.label: College-Level Recommendation
higherEdAlignment.content: Ask students to defend the concept using the chapter language in writing.
